title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and security rather than policy: new work targets behavioral alignment, confidence-aware decision-making, factuality, privacy leakage, and agent security. The strongest fresh items suggest a continued shift from benchmark accuracy toward measuring when models should abstain, how agents fail in tool use, and what post-training changes do to hallucination and memorization risk.

items.title: Systematic security evaluation probes OpenClaw agent frameworks
items.why_it_matters: Agent security failures often emerge from tool use, not base-model behavior alone.

items.title: Google examines behavioral-disposition alignment in LLMs
items.why_it_matters: It pushes alignment evaluation beyond single answers toward more stable behavioral tendencies.

items.title: Decision-theoretic benchmark evaluates when LLMs should answer or abstain
items.why_it_matters: Safer deployment depends on confidence calibration under different risk tolerances.

items.title: New factuality metric emphasizes recall, not just precision, in long-form LLM outputs
items.why_it_matters: It addresses evaluation blind spots where models omit important facts yet still score well.

items.title: Study evaluates and mitigates BibTeX hallucinations in scientific publishing agents
items.why_it_matters: Citation errors are a concrete reliability risk in agentic research workflows.

items.title: Paper studies hallucination effects in reinforcement post-training for multimodal reasoning models
items.why_it_matters: It examines whether post-training gains may mask or amplify multimodal hallucination risks.

items.title: Learned membership inference attack targets memorization in autoregressive language models
items.why_it_matters: Transferable privacy attacks could improve auditing of training-data leakage after fine-tuning.

items.title: Federated learning robustness method aims to resist malicious client attacks
items.why_it_matters: Robust aggregation remains central for secure distributed training under adversarial conditions.

items.title: IoMT intrusion-detection paper targets security and patient-safety risks
items.why_it_matters: Healthcare-connected devices create high-stakes cyber-physical attack surfaces.

items.title: Digital-twin anomaly detection paper focuses on cyberattacks in smart wind-power systems
items.why_it_matters: It reflects growing trust-and-safety attention on AI-enabled critical infrastructure defense.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
